rule.name: Parsing Error
rule.description:
An error occurred when parsing a file. This is an internal error of i-Code CNES analysis.

Please, report this error to i-Code CNES maintainers to improve the tool: https://github.com/lequal/i-CodeCNES/issues

rule.name: COM.DATA.FloatCompare
rule.description: Equality and non equality (.EQ., ==, .NE., /=) are forbidden on reals (REAL, DOUBLE PRECISION, COMPLEX).

rule.name: COM.DATA.Initialisation
rule.description: Variables have to be initialized before being used. When a variable is used, the algorithm should checked if it is initialized and if not throw an error.

rule.name: COM.DATA.Invariant
rule.description: Data declared into subroutine, function, ... and which are never modified then should be declared as constant.

rule.name: COM.DATA.LoopCondition
rule.description: It is forbidden to modify loop condition inside the loop.

rule.name: COM.DATA.NotUsed
rule.description: Every declared variable should be used.

rule.name: COM.DESIGN.Alloc
rule.description: Allocation and deallocation of ressources should be in the same level. For each “DEALLOCATE” keyword found, i-Code CNES checks that the keyword “ALLOCATE” is also found and checks that they are relative to the same resource.

rule.name: COM.DESIGN.ActiveWait
rule.description: In a loop, SLEEP, WAIT and PAUSE instructions are forbidden.

rule.name: COM.FLOW.Abort
rule.description: Keyword STOP is forbidden.

rule.name: COM.FLOW.BooleanExpression
rule.description: In a condition (IF,DO), maximum number of operands is 5 (AND, OR, NEQV, XOR, EQV, NOT, LT, <, LE, <=, GT, >, GE, >=, EQ, ==, NE, /=).

rule.name: COM.FLOW.CheckCodeReturn
rule.description: Function return values should be checked.

rule.name: COM.FLOW.CheckUser
rule.description: The identity of the user running the program should be checked.

rule.name: COM.FLOW.Exit
rule.description: Function, procedure and method should have a unique exit point.

rule.name: COM.FLOW.ExitLoop
rule.description: Function, procedure and method should have a unique exit point.

rule.name: COM.FLOW.FileExistence
rule.description: File opening (OPEN, READ, WRITE) should be done after using INQUIRE and EXIST instruction of this file.

rule.name: COM.FLOW.FilePath
rule.description: In the OPEN instruction, it is forbidden to use directly the file name (file.txt). Path should be defined through a variable.

rule.name: COM.INST.BoolNegation
rule.description: Double negation is forbidden on Boolean expressions. Negations are defined with the keyword « NOT ». Following expressions are not allowed: NOT. (.NOT. a) -> ( a ) .NOT. ( a .AND. .NOT. b) -> .NOT. a .OR. b

rule.name: COM.INST.Brace
rule.description: Every expression should be braced.

rule.name: COM.INST.CodeComment
rule.description: Commented code is forbidden. i-Code CNES checks all keywords in comments, except in the header.

rule.name: COM.INST.GoTo
rule.description: "GO TO" is forbidden.

rule.name: COM.INST.LoopCondition
rule.description: In a loop instruction, comparison using (.EQ., ==, .NE., \=) are forbidden.

rule.name: COM.NAME.Homonymy
rule.description: Variable name should be unique.

rule.name: COM.PROJECT.Header
rule.description: Headers are mandatory for each module and function.

rule.name: COM.PRES.Indent
rule.description: Source code should be indented with spaces: DO i = 2, nb somme = somme + x(i) IF (isnan(somme)) THEN print *, 'somme is a NaN' moy = -1.0 END IF END DO

rule.name: COM.PRES.LengthLine
rule.description: A line of source code should contain 100 characters maximum.

rule.name: COM.TYPE.Expression
rule.description: In a expression (defined by operator like +, -, /, *, **) all variables should have the same type : REAL, INTEGER, …

rule.name: COM.FLOW.CaseSwitch
rule.description: SWITCH instruction should define the DEFAULT case as last one.

rule.name: COM.INST.Line
rule.description: Each line should be limited to only one expression.

rule.name: COM.FLOW.Recursion
rule.description: Recursion is forbidden. In Fortran, i-Code CNES checks that "RECURSIVE FUNCTION(params)" is not found.

rule.name: F90.BLOC.File
rule.description: Every open file should be closed.

rule.name: F90.DATA.Array
rule.description: Array dimensions should be respected.

rule.name: F90.DATA.ArrayAccess
rule.description: In an array of indirections, it is not possible to define the same item several times.

rule.name: F90.DATA.Constant
rule.description: Constants which appear in several subprograms should be defined into a module.

rule.name: F90.DATA.ConstantFloat
rule.description: Numerical constants (see above) should be followed by subtype parameter.

rule.name: F90.DATA.Declaration
rule.description: The use of IMPLICIT NONE instruction is mandatory for each method header. Limitations : this rule is not checked for function and array.

rule.name: F90.DATA.Float
rule.description: It is forbidden to use * output format (WRITE instruction) for REAL.

rule.name: F90.DATA.Parameter
rule.description: Functions SELECTED_REAL_KIND and SELECTED_INT_KIND should be grouped in the same module.

rule.name: F90.DESIGN.Free
rule.description: Allocated memory should be free in the same conceptual level.

rule.name: F90.DESIGN.Include
rule.description: File inclusion is forbidden.

rule.name: F90.DESIGN.Interface
rule.description: Modules should only contains USE, PRIVATE and PUBLIC clauses.

rule.name: F90.DESIGN.IO
rule.description: In an OPEN function, units number should depend on another function or array.

rule.name: F90.DESIGN.Obsolete
rule.description: This rule checks the following points: - Do not use computed GO TO - Do not use syntaxe: CHARACTER*N - Do not use arithmetic IF - In a DO loop, do not use REAL variables, neither as indice, nor as interval limits, nor as increment step - Do not exit a DO loop with other than END DO or CONTINUE - Do not branch on ENDIF - Do not use PAUSE - Do not use assigned GOTO - Do not use FORMAT label - Do not use descriptor H (Hollerith) in formats

rule.name: F90.ERR.Allocate
rule.description: Allocation and deallocation should contain “STAT” parameter. Then, « STAT » value should be tested.

rule.name: F90.ERR.OpenRead
rule.description: OPEN and READ instructions, which work on files, should contain IOSTAT parameter and check this variable value. To check this, OPEN instruction should contain FILE attribute.

rule.name: F90.INST.Associated
rule.description: Between declaration and ASSOCIATED instruction, it is mandatory to call NULLIFY.

rule.name: F90.INST.Entry
rule.description: ENTRY instruction is forbidden.

rule.name: F90.INST.Equivalence
rule.description: EQUIVALENCE instruction is forbidden.

rule.name: F90.INST.If
rule.description: IF followed by one of these instructions : EXIT, GOTO, CYCLE, RETURN is forbidden.

rule.name: F90.INST.Intent
rule.description: Each subprogram parameter should be declared with INTENT keyword.

rule.name: F90.INST.Nullify
rule.description: After deallocate, the use of NULLIFY into the same logical unit is mandatory.

rule.name: F90.INST.Only
rule.description: The use of keyword ONLY without comment is forbidden.

rule.name: F90.INST.Operator
rule.description: Do not use old notation. Replace .EQ., .NE., .LT., .LE., .GT., .GE. by ==, /=, <, <=, >, >=.

rule.name: F90.INST.Pointer
rule.description: POINTER is forbidden except in those cases: - Complex data structure creation - Manage array references - Dynamic allocation into components with derived types

rule.name: F90.NAME.GenericIntrinsic
rule.description: Do not use specific functions (see below), use generic ones: BAD: result = AMOD (argument, divider) GOOD: result = MOD (argument, divider)

rule.name: F90.NAME.KeyWords
rule.description: Fortran keywords should not be used as variable names.

rule.name: F90.PROTO.Overload
rule.description: Overriding operators is forbidden.

rule.name: F90.REF.Array
rule.description: The use of a whole array should be represented using the notation (:).

rule.name: F90.REF.Interface
rule.description: Called subprogram should be visible.

rule.name: F90.REF.Label
rule.description: END keyword should be followed by the type (function, subroutine…) and the name.

rule.name: F90.REF.Open
rule.description: Every OPEN instruction should have parameters FILE, STATUS, IOSTAT and POSITION.

rule.name: F90.REF.Variable
rule.description: A variable should be referenced with the same name in subprogram.

rule.name: F90.TYPE.Derivate
rule.description: Every type declaration should be placed into a module.

rule.name: F90.TYPE.Integer
rule.description: INTEGER parameters should be followed by SELECTED_INT_KIND expression.

rule.name: F90.TYPE.Real
rule.description: REAL parameters should be followed by SELECTED_REAL_KIND.

rule.name: COM.PRES.FileLength
rule.description: The file must be short

rule.name: COM.MET.LineOfCode
rule.description: The number of line of code must be lower

rule.name: COM.FLOW.CheckArguments
rule.description: You must control every arguments

rule.name: COM.MET.ComplexitySimplified
rule.description: The cylcomatic number from Simplified McCabe must be lowered

rule.name: F90.DESIGN.LogicUnit
rule.description: Please refer to RNC Q80.

rule.name: COM.MET.RatioComment
rule.description: You must add more comments on your code

rule.name: COM.PRES.Data
rule.description: Please refer to RNC Q80.

rule.name: F90.FILE.Header
rule.description: You must add an header to this file

rule.name: EUM.BLOC.NamedLoops
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1490: Named loops

Named loops shall be used to aid visual matching of the DO and END DO statements when there are multiple levels of nested loops or when EXIT or CYCLE statements are used.

Example

name: DO iVar = iStart, iFinish [,iStep]
   <statements>
END DO name

Implementation

This rule flags unnamed DO loops when nesting depth is 2 or more, or when EXIT/CYCLE is used inside a DO loop.

rule.name: EUM.BLOC.WhereElse
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1470: WHERE constructs with ELSE WHERE option

WHERE constructs with ELSE WHERE options shall have an empty ELSE WHERE option.

Example

[name:] WHERE (<condition>)
  <statements>
[[ELSE WHERE (<condition>)
  <statements>]
ELSE WHERE
  <statements>]
END WHERE [name]

rule.name: EUM.DESIGN.ModuleStructure
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0180: Module files structure

Each MODULE shall consist of the following components:

  • MODULE xxxx statement
  • Software file header
  • Module dependencies (with USE statement)
  • IMPLICIT NONE statement
  • PRIVATE statement
  • Definition of PUBLIC elements
  • Local variables, constants and types declaration
  • Data initialisation
  • CONTAINS
  • Body of the module, with the codification of all procedures
  • END MODULE (full form)

Implementation

This rule checks for the presence of key required sections: IMPLICIT NONE, PRIVATE, CONTAINS, and END MODULE in full form (not bare END).

rule.name: EUM.DESIGN.NoGlobalVars
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0730: Modules and global variables

MODULEs shall not be used for global variables.

This is bad programming practice and makes maintenance of the source code very difficult.

Implementation

This rule flags modules that contain variable declarations but no CONTAINS section (i.e., pure data modules with no procedures, which likely indicate global variable misuse).

rule.name: EUM.DESIGN.OneUnitPerFile
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0110: Software file content

Each programming unit (PROGRAM or MODULE) shall be in a different file.

Implementation

This rule checks that each file contains only one programming unit (PROGRAM or MODULE). It flags files that contain both a PROGRAM and a MODULE, or more than one MODULE or PROGRAM.

rule.name: EUM.DESIGN.ProgramStructure
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0170: Program files structure

Each PROGRAM shall consist of the following components:

  • PROGRAM xxxx statement
  • Software file header
  • Module dependencies (with USE statement)
  • IMPLICIT NONE statement
  • Local types, variables and constants declarations
  • Data initialisation
  • Body of the program
  • CONTAINS statement (optional)
  • Local subroutines and functions (optional)
  • END PROGRAM statement (full form)

Implementation

This rule checks for the presence of key required sections: IMPLICIT NONE and END PROGRAM in full form (not bare END).

rule.name: EUM.DESIGN.SubroutineStructure
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0190: Subroutines and functions content

A FORTRAN function or subroutine shall consist of the following:

  • Subroutine header
  • Subroutine xxxx definition
  • Declaration of arguments
  • Local definitions
  • Executable block
  • FORMAT statements
  • END SUBROUTINE xxxx statement

Example

!-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
! Name:
! Purpose:
! Argument I/O:
! Returns:
!-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SUBROUTINE mySubroutine( in_rX, in_rY )

!---USE statements--------------------------------------
...
!---End of USE statements-------------------------------

!---Declaration of arguments----------------------------
  REAL, INTENT(IN)           :: in_rX
  REAL, INTENT(IN), OPTIONAL :: in_rY
  ...
!---End of declaration of arguments---------------------

!---Declaration of local variables----------------------
  INTEGER :: iInteger
  REAL    :: rReal
...
!---End of declaration of local variables---------------

!---Executable block------------------------------------
...
!---End of executable block-----------------------------

!---Format block----------------------------------------
...
!---End of format block---------------------------------

END SUBROUTINE mySubroutine

Implementation

This rule checks for the presence of a header before the definition and END SUBROUTINE in full form (not bare END).

rule.name: EUM.FILE.HeaderContent
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0500: Software file header

Each programming unit (PROGRAM or MODULE) shall have a header with, at least, the following components:

  • Unique identification of the module concerned
  • Unique identification of the file
  • Author's name
  • Statement of the Copyright ownership of the code
  • Description - description of the nature of functions/subroutines in this file

Example

!-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
! Component Name:  component name
! File: file name (it may be automatically inserted by the code management tool)
! Author: author name
! Copyright: EUMETSAT 2015
! Description: brief description of the purpose of the file content (e.g. class
!              description)
!-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Implementation

This rule validates that the file header contains the required fields: Component Name, File, Author, Copyright, and Description.

rule.name: EUM.INST.ArgOrder
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0820: Input / Output order

IN parameters shall be passed first, then INOUT parameters, then finally OUT parameters.

rule.name: EUM.INST.ArgTypeDecl
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0810: Input / Output declaration

All input and output arguments shall have declared data types.

Implementation

This rule checks that all dummy arguments (from the SUBROUTINE/FUNCTION signature) have an explicit data type declaration in the procedure body.

rule.name: EUM.INST.Backspace
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1670: No BACKSPACE

BACKSPACE shall not be used.

rule.name: EUM.INST.BlockData
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1100: No BLOCK DATA

BLOCK DATA shall not be used. Use modules instead.

rule.name: EUM.INST.CharLen
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1040: Character string variables

Character string variables shall use the LEN=[number] attribute.

Example

CHARACTER(LEN=128) :: cString

Implementation

This rule checks that character variables use the LEN=n attribute, not the *n notation. For example, character(len=10) is OK, but character*10 is not.

rule.name: EUM.INST.CompilerExt
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1800: Compiler extensions

No compiler- or platform-dependent extensions shall be used.

Implementation

This rule detects the following compiler extension pragmas and non-standard features: !DEC$, !DIR$, !GCC$, BYTE, %VAL, %REF, %LOC.

rule.name: EUM.INST.Continuation
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1810: Continuation Character

The continuation character shall be an ampersand and shall be placed only at the end of each line that is to be continued.

Example

1000 FORMAT (1x, 'APLHA', 5x, 'BETA', 5x, 'GAMMA', &
             5x, 'DELTA')

rule.name: EUM.INST.Continue
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1530: CONTINUE statement

CONTINUE shall not be used.

Implementation

This rule detects standalone CONTINUE statements (not END CONTINUE).

rule.name: EUM.INST.DoubleColon
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1030: Double colon

Double colon (::) shall be used to declare a variable (even without attributes).

Example

INTEGER :: iIndex

Implementation

This rule checks that :: is present in all declarations. For example, integer x is a violation; it must be integer :: x.

rule.name: EUM.INST.DummyArgOrder
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0840: Order of dummy arguments

Declaration of dummy arguments shall follow the same order in which they appear in the subroutine or function calling sequence.

rule.name: EUM.INST.EqvOperators
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1310: Equivalence operators

Logical comparison operators (.EQV., .NEQV.) shall only be used to compare two logical variables. Never compare a logical variable with .TRUE. or .FALSE.

Use of .EQ. and .NE. on LOGICAL operands is not supported, except via compiler options, which is not recommended except for legacy code (where the behaviour expected by the code is assumed).

Implementation

This rule checks that .EQV./.NEQV. are not used with .TRUE./.FALSE. literals.

rule.name: EUM.INST.FormatPlacement
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1690: FORMAT statement placement

FORMAT statements shall be placed at the end of the scope of the corresponding READ or WRITE statements, as described by the rule EUM-FCRule-0190.

rule.name: EUM.INST.FormatStmt
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1680: READ & WRITE associated FORMAT

Format in the operations of reading and writing (READ and WRITE) shall use an associated FORMAT statement (rather than format specification directly in the READ or WRITE statement).

rule.name: EUM.INST.FreeFormatRead
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1700: Space separated data reading

Reading of data shall be based on space separated data and free format READ. The use of formatted data reading should be limited to those cases in which data interpretation cannot be left to the compiler (FORMAT='*').

rule.name: EUM.INST.FunctionIntent
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0930: FUNCTION's calling sequence

The calling sequence arguments of a function shall exclusively be of INTENT(IN) type.

rule.name: EUM.INST.Namelist
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1860: Do not use NAMELIST

NAMELIST shall not be used.

rule.name: EUM.INST.NoData
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1150: No DATA initialization

Initialisation with DATA shall be avoided.

rule.name: EUM.INST.NoLabelledDo
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1480: Labelled loops

Labelled DO loops shall not be used.

Example

      DO label iVar = iStart, iFinish [, iStep]
         <statements>
label CONTINUE

rule.name: EUM.INST.NoSingleLineWhere
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1460: No single line WHERE

Single line WHERE statements shall not be used. Use the form WHERE ... [ELSE WHERE ... ] END WHERE instead.

Example

WHERE (<condition>) <statement>    ! WRONG

[name:] WHERE (<condition>)        ! RIGHT
  <statements>
[[ELSE WHERE (<condition>)
  <statements>]
ELSE WHERE
  <statements>]
END WHERE [name]

rule.name: EUM.INST.NoUnderscoreKind
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1220: No underscore + kind attribute

Qualification of constants and variables by means of underscore + kind attribute shall not be used.

Example

DOUBLE PRECISION :: x
...
y = x_8    ! Not OK

rule.name: EUM.INST.OneVarPerLine
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1010: Only one variable declaration per line

Only one variable shall be declared per source code statement.

Implementation

This rule checks that only one variable is declared per :: statement. For example, integer :: a, b is a violation.

rule.name: EUM.INST.OptionalAfterMandatory
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0850: Dummy arguments after non-optional

OPTIONAL dummy arguments shall be declared after mandatory dummy arguments, following also the IN, INOUT, OUT orders.

rule.name: EUM.INST.OptionalDefault
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0940: OPTIONAL attribute

Use of OPTIONAL attribute shall be restricted to the implementation of default parameters. The corresponding calling parameters must be of INTENT(IN) type and a default value must be assigned at the start of the procedure, if the respective actual parameter is not present in the procedure call. They shall appear after mandatory arguments.

rule.name: EUM.INST.OptionalNamed
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0830: Explicitly identify OPTIONAL arguments

OPTIONAL arguments shall be explicitly identified in procedure calls by the name of the dummy argument associated to the name of the actual calling argument.

Example

SUBROUTINE mySubroutine( x, y )
  REAL, INTENT(IN)           :: x
  REAL, INTENT(IN), OPTIONAL :: y
  ...
CALL mySubroutine( myX, y=myY )

Implementation

This rule checks same-file procedure calls only (subroutine defined in the same file). Cross-file analysis is a candidate for future improvement.

rule.name: EUM.INST.PercentBlank
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1830: Blank usage with %

Blanks shall not be used in identifiers before and after the % record field separator (i.e. DATA%ITEM, not DATA % ITEM).

rule.name: EUM.INST.PureFunc
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0950: PURE SUBROUTINES & FUNCTIONS

In FORTRAN 95, SUBROUTINEs and FUNCTIONs shall have the PURE attribute when possible. It is desirable that subroutines are re-entrant (they have the same behaviour every time they are called).

Implementation

This rule uses a heuristic: if a FUNCTION has all INTENT(IN) arguments and no external I/O (no READ/WRITE), it should be marked PURE. Note: this may have false negatives as not all side effects can be detected.

rule.name: EUM.INST.Redundant
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1850: Redundant features

The use of the following redundant FORTRAN 90 language features shall not be used:

  • The D exponent letter for double precision numbers (e.g. 1.23D-45; use 1.23E-45 instead)
  • Common blocks (use modules instead)
  • Rational operators .EQ., .NE., .LT., .LE., .GT., .GE. (use ==, /=, <, etc instead)
  • CONTINUE statement
  • Statement functions
  • CHARACTER*(*) declaration
  • ENTRY points
  • Real and Double precision DO control variables and DO loop control expressions
  • INCLUDE statements (use USE statement instead)

Note: Most items are already covered by other specific EUM rules; this rule serves as a catch-all.

rule.name: EUM.INST.StringDim
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0870: String dimensions of dummy arguments

Dimensions of character string as dummy arguments shall use the LEN attribute and the asterisk (*) to identify the length. This rule applies even to the cases in which the dimension of the character string is known inside the procedure. The use of asterisk (*) rather than a predefined dimension permits that the character string memory is properly passed to the procedure. Checks on the size of the actual calling argument can be performed inside the subroutine if necessary (with LEN and LEN_TRIM). Providing enough memory for the procedure call is caller responsibility.

Example

SUBROUTINE name (A)
  CHARACTER(LEN=*)   :: A     ! OK
  CHARACTER(LEN=23)  :: A     ! Not OK

rule.name: EUM.MET.MaxArguments
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-2030: Maximum number of arguments

Maximum number of arguments in a procedure shall be 10.

rule.name: EUM.MET.MaxAttributes
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-2040: Maximum number of attributes

Maximum number of attributes in an abstract type shall be 10.

rule.name: EUM.MET.MaxContinuation
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-2050: Maximum number of continuation lines

Maximum number of continuation lines shall be 10 (the maximum allowed in the standard is 39).

rule.name: EUM.MET.MaxProcedures
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-2010: Maximum number of procedures per module

Maximum number of procedures in a module shall be 20.

rule.name: EUM.NAME.Constants
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0410: Constants

All constants shall contain no lower case characters (except the type indicator when using Hungarian notation) and shall be defined with the PARAMETER attribute.

Example

DOUBLE PRECISION, PARAMETER :: TAUCONSTANT = 6.28318530718  ! Simple notation

DOUBLE PRECISION, PARAMETER :: rTAUCONSTANT = 6.28318530718 ! Hungarian notation

rule.name: EUM.NAME.FileExt
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0150: File extension

FORTRAN source files shall have an extension appropriate to the version being used.

Example

.f90, F90, f95, F95, .f03...

Which extension is to be applied is compiler dependent. For example, gfortran treats F90 source files differently to f90.

Implementation

This rule checks that the file extension is in the allowed list: .f90, .F90, .f95, .F95, .f03, .F03. Any other extension is flagged.

rule.name: EUM.NAME.FormatLabels
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1710: FORMAT labels

Labels for FORMAT statements shall start at 1000 and increase by 10 for each statement.

rule.name: EUM.NAME.IdChars
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0320: Identifier Characters

Identifiers shall consist only of the following characters:

  • Upper case alphabetic letters [A...Z]
  • Lower case alphabetic letters [a...z]
  • Digits [0...9]
  • Underscore character

FORTRAN compilers do not distinguish between upper and lower case. Case should be used to aid the readability of the names but cannot be used as the only difference between variable names.

rule.name: EUM.NAME.IdFormat
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0370: Identifiers format

Identifiers shall be defined as [scope_][type]<name> where:

  • [scope_] This is a module identification key plus an underscore. This shall be omitted for identifiers which are local to a subroutine or function. It shall use the following convention:
    KeywordScope
    AAbb_For public MODULE variables
    bb_For private MODULE variables
    N/AFor local to a subroutine or function
    Where the upper-case letters (AA) identify uniquely the library on which the module is located, and the lower-case letters (bb) identify uniquely the module inside the library.
  • [type] Only when using Hungarian notation. It contains one or more lower case letters that identify the type of variable with the following convention, and in the specified order. This is omitted for MODULEs and Functions/Subroutines.
    PrefixTypeComment
    cCharacter
    iInteger
    rReal / Double precision
    xComplex
    lLogical
    tType (Structure)
    aArrayIt can be used in combination with other prefixes.
    pPointerIt can be used in combination with other prefixes.
  • <name> Is a meaningful name. It shall:
    • contain only capital letters for constants (e.g. SOMECONSTANT) or
    • use CamelCase format, starting always with uppercase, for the rest of identifiers (e.g. LoopCounter).
    Underscore characters shall not be used in function or variable meaningful names.

Example

ORbt_iLoopCounter: Integer in the Orbit module.
                   Public MODULE variable
es_arQuaternion:   Array of Real in the Attitude Estimation module.
                   Private MODULE variable
iNumClouds:        Integer counter of number of clouds. Local variable.
iTAUCONSTANT:      Integer constant. Local variable

rule.name: EUM.NAME.IdLength
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0330: Identifier Length

A limitation of 32 characters shall be imposed on all identifier names.

rule.name: EUM.NAME.IdScope
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0400: Identifiers format scope

Identifiers format shall apply to:

  • Modules
  • Functions/subroutines
  • Variables
  • Types and structures

Implementation

This is a meta-rule ensuring that EUM.NAME.IdFormat applies to Modules, Functions/subroutines, Variables, and Types/structures. It is implemented as part of the EUM.NAME.IdFormat checker.

rule.name: EUM.NAME.ModuleName
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0130: MODULE names

The name of the MODULE shall follow the format AAbb_Name. Where:

  • The two upper-case letters (AA) shall identify uniquely the library on which the module is located.
  • The lower-case letters (bb) shall identify uniquely the module inside the library.
  • Name shall be a meaningful identifier, using CamelCase notation.

Example

File CLvi_CloudVisualization.f90

MODULE CLvi_CloudVisualization
...
END MODULE

rule.name: EUM.NAME.PrivateFormat
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0390: Private elements format

All elements in the module scope (PRIVATE) should be only prefixed with the module identification, followed by an underscore.

Example

mm_myModuleConstant

rule.name: EUM.NAME.ProgramName
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0120: PROGRAM names

The name of the PROGRAM shall be a meaningful identifier, using CamelCase notation.

Example

File Orbit.f90

PROGRAM Orbit
...
END PROGRAM

rule.name: EUM.NAME.PublicFormat
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0380: Public elements format

All PUBLIC elements within a module should be prefixed in the same manner as the module they belong to.

Example

LLmm_iMyPublicVariable

rule.name: EUM.PRES.BlankLines
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0610: Blank lines

Blank lines shall be placed wherever they improve the appearance and layout of the code.

Implementation

This rule checks that there are no more than 2 consecutive blank lines.

rule.name: EUM.PRES.BlockAlign
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0240: Control blocks justification

Control blocks shall be properly aligned. DO and END DO starting at the same column and the lines in between consistently indented to the right.

rule.name: EUM.PRES.CommentBlock
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0590: Comment blocks

Comment blocks shall be placed at the start of blocks of calculations.

Comment blocks are one or more lines of text. They should typically be placed at the start of blocks of calculations, DO ... END DO loops, or larger IF ... END IF constructs. They should also be included wherever they will serve to clarify some lines of code.

Example

!  Check all plates are within dimension limits
   lDimsok = .TRUE.
   DO iLoop = 1, iNumPlates
      rXpos = arCoord (1,iLoop)
      rYpos = arCoord (2,iLoop)
      rZpos = arCoord (3,iLoop)
      CALL CheckDim (rXpos, rZpos, lDimsok)
   END DO
!  iDimsok is now false if limits were exceeded.

Implementation

This rule flags big (5 or more lines) IF/DO constructs that are not preceded by a comment. Smaller constructs (1 line or less) are skipped.

rule.name: EUM.PRES.CommentPos
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0520: Comment positions

Comments shall be placed at the following positions:

  • At the beginning of each iterative process or loop, as described by EUM-FCRule-0590.
  • Preceding a series of lines of code that make a clearly defined function
  • Preceding any logical process (IF, WHERE, SELECT CASE...)
  • Before calling a subroutine or function
  • Preceding any sentence or block of sentences for reading and writing
  • Declaration of variables (grouped by related functionality), as described by EUM-FCRule-0600.

Implementation

This rule flags IF/DO/SELECT CASE/CALL/READ/WRITE statements not preceded by a comment line. Single-line comments count. Nearby violations are grouped to reduce noise.

rule.name: EUM.PRES.Doxygen
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0580: Doxygen comments

Doxygen comment codes should be used when declaring a function.

Example

!> comment preceding function
  FUNCTION XXmm_MyFunction( x ) RESULT( res )

Implementation

This rule checks that !> is used before function declarations. The !< notation is NOT allowed.

rule.name: EUM.PRES.IndentLevel
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0220: Indentation level

Each level of indentation shall consist of two blank spaces.

rule.name: EUM.PRES.LabelJustify
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0230: Labels justification

Labels shall be left justified to the same column to facilitate the browsing.

Implementation

This rule checks that all labels (statement numbers like 1000, 100) start at column 1.

rule.name: EUM.PRES.NoCommentMultiLine
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0550: No comments between multi-line sentences

Comments shall not be inserted between sentences that are constituted by more than a line (with continuation lines).

rule.name: EUM.PRES.NoEmptyComment
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0570: Do not use empty comment lines

Empty comment lines shall not be used. Use empty lines instead.

rule.name: EUM.PRES.NoEndLineComment
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0560: Do not use end-of-line comments

End-of-line comments (comments at the end of code lines) shall not be used.

rule.name: EUM.PRES.NoTabs
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0210: No TABS

The TAB character shall not be used. Use blank characters for indentation and spacing instead.

rule.name: EUM.PROJECT.HeaderContent
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-0510: Subroutines and function header

Each programming function or subroutine shall have a header with the following components:

  • Routine name
  • Purpose of the routine
  • Description of the input/output argument
  • Description of the value returned

Example

!-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
! Name: function name
! Purpose: brief description of the purpose of the function
! Argument I/O: input/output arguments and affected global data with a brief
!               explanation of their meaning
! Returns: a brief explanation of the meaning of the returned value
!-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Implementation

This rule validates that the procedure header contains the required fields: Name, Purpose, Argument I/O, and Returns.

rule.name: EUM.TYPE.PrivateInType
rule.description:
EUM-FCRule-1160: Abstract Types Declaration

Abstract types shall always contain the PRIVATE statement.

This is a good Object-Oriented practice to avoid incorrect set/get of the data.

Example

TYPE AAbb_tStateVector
  PRIVATE
  DOUBLE PRECISION :: rEpoch
  DOUBLE PRECISION, DIMENSION(6) :: daData
END TYPE AAbb_tStateVector
